message:
fix(lab): record correct severity in replay trace for cancelled tasks

The replay recorder was calling record_task_completed() with the
severity from the raw future outcome BEFORE the task state was
finalized. Since create_task wraps user futures to always return
Outcome::Ok(()), the raw outcome severity was always Ok — even for
tasks that went through the cancel protocol and should be recorded
as Cancelled.

Fix: Move record_task_completed() AFTER the task state finalization
block, and derive the severity from the finalized TaskState instead
of the raw outcome. For tasks in TaskState::Completed(outcome), use
outcome.severity(); for all other states, default to Severity::Ok.

This ensures the replay trace accurately reflects the cancel protocol
state machine's determination — cancelled tasks are recorded with
Cancelled severity, panicked tasks with Panicked severity, etc.

Updated the regression test (replay_records_correct_severity_for_cancelled_task)
to properly exercise the cancel protocol path:
- Task now yields once to allow cancellation
- Explicit request_cancel + acknowledge_cancel to enter Cancelling state
- Reschedule and run_until_quiescent for the cancel protocol to
  finalize the task as Completed(Cancelled)
- Added assertion that the task reaches Completed(Cancelled) state
  before checking the replay trace

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
